name: Knowledge-gated revisit

name: Multi-solution state graph
quarantineReason: The submitted works close different specializations: Four Swords Adventures closes an aggregate-contribution threshold and Echoes of Wisdom closes object-role composition. Their common denominator admits a single mandatory construction and therefore does not discriminate a multi-solution pattern. Removing complete paths, equivalent starts, a shared postcondition, causal independence, and non-domination while testing only one successful construction would make the released multi-solution stable ID semantically false; the original name, subtype, principle, and identity remain unchanged and non-selectable.
promotionRequirements: Require at least two independently viable complete paths from controlled comparable starts to the same explicit postcondition in each of two distinct works. Before promotion, add formal generalizes or specializes relations and reciprocal precedence plus collision fixtures against object-role-composition, systemic-verb-toolkit, scalar aggregate thresholds, one mandatory two-input construction, and one prescribed signature-tool route; then execute rollback and route fixtures and renew individual and combination originality review.

teach: Commit the reward transaction and demonstrate one safe function with explicit persistent-state feedback.

practice: Present a later-use context in a second functional dimension.

Recovery: Keep the persistent state set and allow the context to be retried.
variation: Present another context that changes function surface without duplicating a functional dimension.
Success evidence: The player transfers the payoff's broader value without a copied source pairing.
Recovery: Hint the functional dimension and feedback channel only.
combineOrReverse: Combine a declared function with an existing verb or temporarily suppress one context without deleting persistent state.
Success evidence: Derived availability changes coherently while the acquisition transaction remains committed.
Recovery: Reconstruct all function bindings from persistent state on load or rollback.
exam: Use at least two distinct functions in separate future-use contexts after save or travel.
Success evidence: Both contexts show positive before-and-after actionability deltas from the same acquisition transaction's state set.
Recovery: Restore the last coherent checkpoint and re-derive function availability.

navigationInvariants: Every failed step returns to current safe anchor
Opening completion exposes exactly one clear immediate direction plus optional inspection

routeRequirements: Every failed step returns to current safe anchor
Opening completion exposes exactly one clear immediate direction plus optional inspection
cloneRiskChecks: source storm rescue and castle approach
source castaway weapon recovery
source first cave, mentor, or iconic item staging
Independent blind reviewers must not converge on one source encounter with high confidence.

teach: Present one urgent, readable target and a safe verb whose consequence is immediate.
Success evidence: Player performs the verb and can state what changed.
Recovery: Reset only the local target and repeat contextual prompt.
practice: Ask for the same verb once more while an actor or environment responds to the player's responsibility.
Success evidence: Player acts without full prompt.
Recovery: Use a short situated cue, not a controls panel.
variation: Apply the opening responsibility to a second target with a changed spatial relation.
Success evidence: Player transfers control and role expectation.
Recovery: Highlight target affordance after a delay.
combineOrReverse: Chain two already introduced verbs or force a brief retreat that still serves the immediate need.
Success evidence: Player understands the action loop rather than one button.
Recovery: Restart at the current chain step with prior steps preserved.
exam: Resolve or transform the immediate need and infer the next goal from the consequence.
Success evidence: Player can name role, present situation, and next direction.
Recovery: Offer an optional factual recap and reopen local inspection.

fallbacks: Use a compact three-node opening with contextual prompts, immediate state changes, and a post-action dialogue line that confirms role and next goal.
accessibility: Allow prompt persistence and input remapping
Offer no-fail or slowed opening timing
Caption urgent offscreen cues
Permit replayable role-and-goal recap after action

failureModes: Controls drill detached from story
Urgency prevents orientation
Dialogue explains identity before action
Opening action has punitive failure
recoveryRules: Restart current step only
Reduce urgency without changing canon outcome
Reframe target in camera
Provide optional factual recap after attempt

retainedPrinciples: The opening should teach control, player role, and immediate story direction through one motivated chain of safe actions.
forbiddenSurfaceTransfers: source storm rescue and castle approach
source castaway weapon recovery
source first cave, mentor, or iconic item staging
cloneRiskChecks: source storm rescue and castle approach
source castaway weapon recovery
source first cave, mentor, or iconic item staging
Veto pattern.local-rule-development-arc + pattern.landmark-promises-future-use when Opening emergency, route shape, first verb or item, mentor function, and next landmark align with one source title.

counterEvidenceBounds.challengedAssumption: A readable foundational verb automatically constitutes action-first onboarding.
counterEvidenceBounds.observedBoundary: A versatile combat control principle can improve the whole game without proving that the opening action teaches player role, immediate need, and story direction.
counterEvidenceBounds.derivedRule: Use a verb-teaching or combat-readability pattern when the action does not carry opening narrative identity.

intent.designProblem: An overworld should contain authored rule development rather than serve only as travel between isolated challenges.
intent.principle: The overworld can be the primary challenge space when its connections continuously demand observation, traversal, resource, encounter, and route decisions.

precedence: Priority 81: Select when connective space itself sustains traversal, resource, puzzle, encounter, and route decisions; use constrained vehicle when a lane network is the authored challenge.
Evaluate before pattern.landmark-promises-future-use.
contraindications: Overworld is mostly empty travel between isolated stages
A single rail or lane determines all progress
Density destroys landmark readability or recovery
Region layout or encounter chain copies a source field
$.stageEvidence.overworldRegions[*].topology equals "linear-network"
$.gameplayPlan.overworldRegions[*].travelOnlyCellRatio greater-than 0.5
$.stageEvidence.overworldRegions[*].challengeDensity less-than 0.35
abstainWhen: Overworld is mostly empty travel between isolated stages
A single rail or lane determines all progress
Density destroys landmark readability or recovery
Region layout or encounter chain copies a source field

preconditions: $.player.position must satisfy inside-ref against reference parameter.dense-overworld.region before the authored transaction is accepted.
$.world.regionGraphLoaded must satisfy equals against false before the authored transaction is accepted.
postconditions: $.world.activeConnectionGraph must satisfy equals-ref against reference parameter.dense-overworld.connection-graph before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

$.world.activeRecoveryNodes must satisfy contains-all-ref against reference parameter.dense-overworld.recovery-nodes before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

$.navigation.visibleApproachCount must satisfy greater-or-equal against 2 before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

preconditions: $.player.currentCell must satisfy in-ref-array against reference parameter.dense-overworld.challenge-cells before the authored transaction is accepted.
$.world.currentCell.entryStateValid must satisfy equals against true before the authored transaction is accepted.
postconditions: $.world.currentCell.outcomeState must satisfy in against ["bypassed","solved","depleted","transformed"] before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

$.navigation.connectionPurposeRealized must satisfy equals against true before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.
$.routes.requiredRecovery must satisfy reachable against true before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

preconditions: $.player.traversedConnection must satisfy belongs-to-ref against reference parameter.dense-overworld.connection-graph before the authored transaction is accepted.
postconditions: $.knowledge.region.discoveredConnections must satisfy contains-current against true before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.
$.navigation.returnRouteToRecovery must satisfy exists against true before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

$.telemetry.routeChoiceTradeoffRecorded must satisfy equals against true before the authored transaction is accepted. This realizes the distinct interconnected-traversal-challenge-field causal contract.

criteria.rule: Reward connections that change risk, knowledge, resource, or approach rather than only distance.
Require traversal plus at least two of observation, manipulation, encounter, resource, stealth, or social play.
Reward readable alternate approaches with distinct tradeoffs.
Require recovery and narrative orientation nodes amid continuous challenge.

hardVetoes: Overworld is mostly empty travel between isolated stages
A single rail or lane determines all progress
Density destroys landmark readability or recovery
Region layout or encounter chain copies a source field

constraints: Repeated traversal justified
Boundaries and landmark hierarchy defined
At least three verb categories across set
Each cell has entry, outcome, and recovery state
At least two alternate approaches
Connection purposes annotated
Every challenge cluster reaches one
Narrative orientation or resource reset defined

canonInvariants: Every cluster has recovery
No single verb solves every connection
Travel-only cells remain below density ceiling

revealConstraints: Reveal approach categories before exact solutions
Use landmarks and consequences instead of objective trails
npcTactics: Stage actor tactics so that Make crossing the world feel like continuous thinking and discovery, not downtime between content.
interactionWindows: Player may observe before committing to each cluster
Breathing nodes support optional story response without trapping movement
cueIntents: Make crossing the world feel like continuous thinking and discovery, not downtime between content.
regional landmark promise
short challenge clusters
breathing node
changed return read
skipReplayAccessibility: Skipping or replaying a cue must commit the same authoritative world facts.
Essential causal information remains available through a replayable original-language log.

topologyChanges: Connection graph contains loops or cross-links
Each cluster offers at least one alternate approach or bypass
Recovery node lies within bounded traversal cost
actorBlocking: Use terrain, actors, hazards, and props to express route tradeoffs before interaction.
cameraTransitions: Preserve dominant landmark orientation while letting local occlusion create discovery.
navigationInvariants: Every required destination reachable through at least one supported verb set
Every challenge failure reaches recovery
No alternate approach collapses into identical cost and outcome

routeRequirements: Every required destination reachable through at least one supported verb set
Every challenge failure reaches recovery
No alternate approach collapses into identical cost and outcome
cloneRiskChecks: source overworld geography and landmark placements
source climb-glide, sky-surface-depth, or revisited-province challenge chain
source enemy camps, shrines, towers, tracks, or field reward cadence
Independent blind reviewers must not converge on one source encounter with high confidence.

teach: Build a compact region where a landmark, one challenge cell, and a nearby recovery node are simultaneously readable.
Success evidence: Player crosses using one supported verb and can return safely.
Recovery: Recovery node resets local hazard and reframes the landmark.
practice: Offer two connections to the same intermediate goal with distinct risk, resource, or information tradeoffs.
Success evidence: Player makes and can explain a route choice.
Recovery: Both approaches loop back to a known junction.
variation: Change one connective landform with a new local rule or world state on revisit.
Success evidence: Player re-reads known topology instead of following a new corridor.
Recovery: Stable landmark and one unchanged connection preserve orientation.
combineOrReverse: Link traversal, environmental manipulation, encounter avoidance, and resource recovery across a multi-cell loop, or enter a known cluster vertically from a new layer.
Success evidence: Player composes verbs and discovers a shortcut.
Recovery: Partial outcomes persist only when they cannot strand the player.
exam: Let the player author a route across a dense region toward a story goal while balancing risk, secrets, resources, and breathing opportunities.
Success evidence: Multiple viable route traces reach the goal with meaningfully different costs.
Recovery: Known recovery nodes and reversible cell transactions prevent dead ends.

fallbacks: Represent the region as an illustrated node graph with short playable challenge segments, explicit connection tradeoffs, persistent discoveries, and bounded recovery nodes.
accessibility: Offer optional landmark compass and traveled-path trace
Provide reduced encounter and traversal intensity
Never communicate route danger by color alone
Allow return-to-known-recovery without losing committed discoveries

failureModes: Density becomes clutter
Empty traversal dominates
All routes have identical cost
Recovery is too distant
recoveryRules: Collapse redundant no-choice cells
Add loopback shortcut to known junction
Preserve discovered links across failure
Return to nearest verified recovery node

retainedPrinciples: The overworld can be the primary challenge space when its connections continuously demand observation, traversal, resource, encounter, and route decisions.
forbiddenSurfaceTransfers: source overworld geography and landmark placements
source climb-glide, sky-surface-depth, or revisited-province challenge chain
source enemy camps, shrines, towers, tracks, or field reward cadence
cloneRiskChecks: source overworld geography and landmark placements
source climb-glide, sky-surface-depth, or revisited-province challenge chain
source enemy camps, shrines, towers, tracks, or field reward cadence

Veto pattern.landmark-promises-future-use + pattern.optional-secret-readability + pattern.state-axis-rewrites-routes when Macro geography, landmark hierarchy, traversal verb set, secret distribution, and region-state progression converge on one source overworld.

counterEvidenceBounds.challengedAssumption: Every expanding overworld network is a dense connective challenge field.
counterEvidenceBounds.observedBoundary: A linear network can gain destinations and branch decisions while remaining a constrained-route system rather than traversable challenge space between nodes.
counterEvidenceBounds.derivedRule: Select constrained-vehicle-route when movement is bound to authored lanes or rails and off-network adjacency is not playable.

teach: A future maturity contract must define one safe, observable introduction.
practice: A future maturity contract must define deliberate rule confirmation.
variation: A future maturity contract must distinguish repetition from variation.
combineOrReverse: A future maturity contract must state composition precedence and vetoes.
exam: A future maturity contract must define a no-new-rule examination and recovery path.

fallbacks: No automatic fallback is allowed while this record remains quarantined.
accessibility: Future applications must provide non-color feedback and bounded retry time.

failureModes: Premature production selection would convert an unreviewed analogy into a game rule.
recoveryRules: Remove the record from composition and return to story-derived design.

retainedPrinciples: Separate private and shared views only when communication itself is a supported verb and no player becomes a passive information terminal.
forbiddenSurfaceTransfers: Source names, characters, lore, dialogue, maps, room layouts, object sequences, UI, music, and audiovisual signatures.
cloneRiskChecks: Future release requires independent blind attribution and at least four material transformation axes.

counterEvidenceBounds.challengedAssumption: The current abstraction for Asymmetric screen information may generalize beyond the evidence actually reviewed.
counterEvidenceBounds.observedBoundary: Research-only until a second distinct work and a supported multi-screen runtime provide evidence and validation.
counterEvidenceBounds.derivedRule: Keep Asymmetric screen information outside production retrieval until its boundary and implementation contract pass independent review.

changelog: 1.0.0 — retained as a non-selectable reviewed research record after the first P4 release audit.
